intro: Neoschumannia cardinea, also known as African Mahogany, is a medicinal plant that belongs to the family Achariaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that help to alleviate various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Neoschumannia cardinea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Neoschumannia cardinea is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
